headline: Elysée Palace searched as part of investigation into events contracts
description: The judiciary is examining how several public contracts were awarded to Shortcut Events, the company responsible in recent years for organizing major events for the president. Two investigating judges went to the palace on Thursday.

headline: Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
Canal+ 'blacklist': Over half of recent French films credit people now threatened with retribution
Why Airbus, Air France were convicted on appeal in 2009 Rio-Paris crash
description: The two aviation giants were convicted of manslaughter in the accident of flight AF447, which killed 228 people on June 1, 2009. Both companies had previously been found not guilty at the first trial. They are now filing an appeal with France's highest court.

headline: Major immigration study shows 'great blending' is underway in France, but inequalities persist
description: The second edition of a large-scale sociological survey reveals a rapid convergence in the cultural and social practices of immigrants and their descendants with those of the wider population. However, ongoing difficulties in access to employment and housing confirm the experience of systemic discrimination and racism.

headline: War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two different worlds just 10 kilometers apart
description: On the front line, the small town of Oleshky in the south has been occupied by the Russian military since the invasion began. Residents try to reach Kherson at great personal risk.

headline: Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
description: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

headline: France remains top destination for foreign investment as Europe declines

Trump says US will send 5,000 troops to Poland
How France's pre-deportation centers became an 'extension of the prison system'
Trump's giant arch approved by ally-controlled commission
NGOs sue French state over failure to tackle 'forever chemicals'
Mayors call for cancellation of Patrick Bruel concerts as singer accused of sexual violence
US 'pauses' arms sales to Taiwan over Middle East war
French Open draw sets up tough matches for French players
France's Constitutional Council overturns decision to scrap low-emission zones
These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.

headline: 'Which jobs have the most openings? The least valued ones'
'In a country as polarized as Spain, it is Pedro Sánchez's international stance that garners the most support'
'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
